events.title: Transcribe meeting audio
events.summary: The user transcribed multiple meeting recordings, including 'Meeting 2026-06-06 22:31' and 'Meeting 2026-06-18-20-10', using My Meeting. They compared FluidAudio 30 segments and ONNX Silero 91 segments, noting an 80% transcription success rate.

events.summary: The user addressed 'AudioError error 6' and 'noSpeech' during transcription of 'The Daily Show' on May 28th. They interacted with 'Personal ai meeting 1.m4a' and managed RMS energy and bypass permissions for 'LocalizedError'.

They committed the change with hash 242505e and confirmed successful compilation. The fix maintained visibility of the '新建' (New) button and preloaded connected provider models to avoid delays.

events.title: Resolve model cache clearing bug
events.summary: The user debugged and fixed an issue where providerModelsCache was unexpectedly cleared during expansion in MyMeeting/Views/SettingsView.swift. The root cause was a merged guard condition that triggered cache clearing; they separated the guard logic to preserve pre-loaded models. The fix was tested with connected AI providers like OpenAI and Claude.

events.title: Improve auto-summarize message handling
events.summary: The user addressed bugs in the auto-summarize feature of My Meeting, including tail truncation and cross-group resubmission issues. They tested provider and model dropdowns to ensure only connected and enabled services were listed. The session involved debugging message extraction logic and evaluating whether to continue noise reduction or stop at manual rejection.

events.title: Ensure proper model loading in UI
events.summary: The user fixed asynchronous model loading in the Models page of My Meeting, ensuring the 'RECENTS' sidebar stays visible when collapsed. They preloaded connected provider models to prevent delays during expansion and committed the changes. The fix was validated with API integrations including Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini.

events.title: Fix message handling in multi-event sends
events.summary: The user resolved message loss during boundary sends in My Meeting, addressing endValue and bestGroupText handling. They fixed a critical issue in message processing identified by commit 6143423, ensuring proper handling across multi-event scenarios. The fix supported head avatar grid testing and API provider integration.

events.title: Enable full-row clickability for AI providers
events.summary: The user made each AI provider row in My Meeting's settings fully clickable, including name, icon, and blank space, while ensuring the enable/disable switch only toggles status without expanding. They fixed a UI inconsistency in SettingsView.swift that caused unintended expansion behavior.

events.title: Fix memory visualization pipeline
events.summary: The user fixed the 'writing-capture' pipeline in a macOS app by reverting a 'boundary-send' change to staging, ensuring it now correctly processes 194 records. This was verified by rebuilding in Xcode and running the 'all' tests.

events.title: Fix colored dots in right-click menu
events.summary: The user resolved an issue in 'MemoriesView.swift' where color-coded dots in the right-click menu were appearing gray due to macOS menu styling. They implemented a fix using pre-rendered NSImages to ensure correct foregroundStyle rendering and confirmed the visual change in Xcode.

events.title: Commit color fix and config changes
events.summary: The user committed changes to 'MemoriesView.swift', 'MyMeeting.swift', and 'ConfigStore.swift' with the message 'fix(memories): 改色菜单圆点显示对应颜色 + Default 括号改 random'. The commit hash was 25427ae, and changes included 'Image(nsImage: ...)', 'Default(random)', and 'ProviderKind.from(id)'.

events.title: Fix message boundary splitting
events.summary: The user addressed issues in message processing by refining algorithms for splitting consecutive messages and handling send markers, including 'isSendClear' events and 'submit' actions, within Obsidian 1.12.7. This focused on stabilizing the 'message boundary + consequtive split' logic.

events.title: Implement deterministic input pipeline
events.summary: The user developed a deterministic algorithm for user input collection in Obsidian 1.12.7, focusing on 'Pass 3 · AX 分支 | 确定性算法' and handling 'unit-session' and 'record' structures. The work included managing 'element_hash不切割' and 'rawSessions' for consistent input capture.

events.title: Optimize OCR and LLM routing
events.summary: The user refined LLM routing between 'route=ax' (chat) and 'route=ocr' (document) modes in Obsidian 1.12.7, adjusting parameters like 'windowCharBudget=25k' and processing Canvas files. This included handling 'U+FEFF' and OCR text extraction from Google Docs.

events.title: Enhance document fusion and backlog handling
events.summary: The user worked on document fusion and backlog processing in Obsidian 1.12.7, using OCR modules, LLM embeddings, and compression for raw sessions and edit logs. The session involved managing 'ax_cleaned' and 'ax_paste' files and handling window 655 data.

events.title: Debug input method configurations
events.summary: The user debugged Pinyin, Direct English, and Wubi input methods in Terminal, focusing on edit_log, IME-switching, and portrait/writing_style.md. They ran claude, sourcekit-lsp, and caffeinate commands, testing Latin letters commit, CJK replacement, and code input behaviors.

events.title: Research headless IME feasibility
events.summary: The user explored headless IME control on macOS using Terminal, running 'writing-capture-researcher — caffeinate · claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'. They analyzed focus stealing risks and tested with models '115x29' and 'sly-skip-permissions' to assess background process viability.

events.title: Configure Librime for Chinese typing
events.summary: The user configured and tested the Librime input method engine for Chinese typing, integrating dual-pinyin schemes like 'cangjie5', 'quick5', 'stroke', and 'terra_pinyin'. They used schema and switcher settings, and tested AI providers including Claude and Groq.

events.title: Analyze Rime API structure
events.summary: The user examined Rime API components such as RimeContext, RimeCommit, and RimeMenu in Terminal, locating RIME_STRUCT_INIT and data_size in rime_api.h. They also verified AI provider configurations including OpenAI, Groq, and others.

events.title: Test Rime-ice dictionary integration
events.summary: The user tested the Rime-ice (WuSong Pinyin) dictionary for context-aware pinyin input, feeding personal writing records into librime to customize word libraries. They managed AI providers like groq, openrouter, and ollama with settings for models, connections, and training.

events.title: Validate headless input method pipeline
events.summary: The user validated a headless Rime input method setup with 91% baseline accuracy for 雾凇 (Frosted), using 'writing-capture-researcher' and feeding common phrases into the system. They confirmed the pipeline worked without code changes, referencing /tmp/rime-test and writing_records.

events.title: Test 91% accuracy baseline system
events.summary: The user tested the 雾凇基线 (Frost Baseline) system with 91% accuracy, using '喂你语料补常用词' to feed common phrases into the system. They confirmed memory storage in /tmp/rime-test and discussed potential LLM fusion, referencing 'scratch' and 'writing-capture-researcher'.

events.title: Confirm headless Rime setup
events.summary: The user tested a headless Rime configuration that does not steal focus, achieving 91% accuracy for 雾凇. They verified the workflow was functional without code changes, using /tmp/rime-test and testing phrases like '全是你' and '更新记忆里这条'.

events.title: Validate headless pipeline on Spotify
events.summary: The user validated the headless, non-focus-grabbing Rime input method on Spotify, confirming 91% baseline accuracy for the 'WuSong' model. They tested the full pipeline using 'writing-capture-researcher' and /tmp/rime-test, with no code modifications.

events.title: Verify feed common words feature
events.summary: The user verified the '喂你语料补常用词' feature in the Frost Baseline system, achieving 91% accuracy. They confirmed memory storage in /tmp/rime-test and discussed LLM integration, referencing 'scratch' and 'writing-capture-researcher'.

events.title: Configure librime with permission bypass
events.summary: The user configured librime using Terminal commands like 'caffeinate', 'claude', and 'sly-skip-permissions'. They referenced rime-test/cands.c and #include <rime_api.h>, and discussed integrating writing records into userdb.

events.title: Compile C program for librime
events.summary: The user compiled a C program in /tmp/rime-test, linking against Librime libraries. They referenced lattice.c, rime_api.h, and stdio.h, and discussed integrating Pass1 and AxCleanup models for a potential fusion payload system.

events.title: Test Rime API with C code
events.summary: The user tested a C program called 'lattice.c' in Terminal, compiling it with clang and linking against librime. They modified the code to filter valid CJK hanzi in 3-byte UTF-8 sequences and tested with 'topN_for' for Rime session inputs.

events.title: Integrate librime into My-Portrait
events.summary: The user integrated librime into the My-Portrait project, linking via brew and exploring self-contained packaging. They referenced project.yml, Package.swift, and directories like ~/.portrait and vendor/, while handling permissions and runtime configs.

events.title: Configure Rime for multiple languages
events.summary: The user configured Rime for Chinese (rime-ice, librime, luna_pinyin, 中文全拼), Japanese, and mixed-language input. They used 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' and tested 'writing-capture-researcher', referencing video.mp4 and text_overlay_demo.mp4.

events.title: Implement per-user userdb settings
events.summary: The user implemented per-user userdb and custom dictionary settings for double-pinyin and Wubi input schemes. They parameterized RimeEngine to support multiple input methods, using 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' and 'sly-skip-permissions'.
